steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content:
The passage says Cather portrays Alexandra as having a deep emotional connection to her natural surroundings. We need a quotation that shows inner feeling or attachment to nature — not practical skill, not action, not interaction with other people.
  • Choice A — “She had never known before how much the country meant to her...”: Shows Alexandra’s interior emotional bond — the land “meant” something to her, her “heart were hiding” in the grass, she “felt the future stirring.” ✓
  • Choice B — “Alexandra talked to the men about their crops...”: Describes her interacting with neighbors to learn about farming — practical, social, no emotional connection to nature. ✗
  • Choice C — “Alexandra drove off alone...”: Depicts her driving at night; describes physical action with no mention of her emotional state. ✗
  • Choice D — “It was Alexandra who read the papers and followed the markets...”: Shows her business acumen and market knowledge — again practical, not emotional connection to nature. ✗

steps.title: Select the matching choice
steps.content: Only Choice A gives language of interior feeling: the country “meant” something to her, her heart was “hiding” in the landscape, and she “felt” the future in the ridges — all direct evidence of emotional connection to her surroundings. That matches choice A — A is correct.
